predictions.text: Amazon will price and settle an investment-grade bond offering with aggregate principal amount of at least $35.0 billion.
predictions.verification_criteria: Confirmed by (a) Amazon/AWS press release, (b) SEC filing (8-K, prospectus supplement, or 10-Q disclosure), or (c) lead-manager transaction terms reported by Bloomberg/WSJ/FT showing total issued principal >= $35.0B and settlement completed by the deadline.
monitoring_signals: SEC EDGAR: AMZN 8-K / prospectus supplements
Lead-manager announcements and deal-pricing headlines
Credit-spread and new-issue concession commentary in IG primary market recaps
predictions.rationale: A large announced financing window plus ongoing capex/AI spending makes a jumbo IG issuance likely; the threshold is set below the reported 'up to' amount to reflect partial take-down risk.

predictions.text: Amazon (or AWS) will publish a public post-incident update for the March 2026 outage that explicitly introduces a new control requiring two-person approval (or senior-engineer signoff) for production deployments involving AI-assisted code changes.
predictions.verification_criteria: A publicly accessible Amazon/AWS statement (official blog, postmortem, press release, or updated public engineering policy) that (i) references the March 2026 outage and (ii) states a new deployment control requiring two-person approval or senior signoff specifically tied to AI-assisted changes.
monitoring_signals: AWS 'Security Bulletins' / 'Service health' postmortems
Amazon engineering/blog posts referencing change-management controls
Earnings-call prepared remarks mentioning process changes after the outage
predictions.rationale: Reported internal tightening often becomes formalized after high-profile outages, but public disclosure is less consistent, lowering confidence.

predictions.text: Oracle will report year-over-year growth of at least 35% in its Cloud Infrastructure (IaaS) revenue in at least one quarterly earnings release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Oracle quarterly earnings release and/or accompanying 10-Q/10-K explicitly reporting OCI/IaaS revenue growth >= 35% YoY for the reported quarter (as stated by Oracle).
monitoring_signals: Oracle earnings releases and transcripts
OCI capacity expansion announcements (datacenters, GPU clusters)
Large OCI customer wins tied to AI training/inference
predictions.rationale: Recent AI-driven acceleration supports high growth, but base effects and capacity constraints can cause quarter-to-quarter variability.

predictions.text: Meta will publish a transparency/integrity or threat report that includes a dedicated section discussing 'AI agents' or 'synthetic accounts' and discloses removal/actioning of at least 10,000 accounts for coordinated inauthentic behavior involving AI-generated personas.
predictions.verification_criteria: A publicly posted Meta report (e.g., CIB report, Integrity/Transparency report, threat report) that (i) contains an explicit section discussing AI agents/synthetic accounts and (ii) states Meta removed/disabled/actioned >= 10,000 accounts tied to the described behavior/campaign(s).
monitoring_signals: Meta newsroom posts and quarterly integrity reports
Disclosures from Meta’s threat-intel teams
Platform policy updates on labeling AI-controlled accounts
predictions.rationale: Meta’s increased exposure to agent ecosystems raises reporting pressure, but numeric thresholds and topic-specific disclosure are uncertain.

predictions.text: The Social Security Administration (SSA) will issue a public breach notification (or report to Congress publicly summarized) stating that at least 500,000 individuals were affected by unauthorized access/exfiltration of Social Security data tied to the reported thumb-drive incident.
predictions.verification_criteria: SSA (or an official federal notice) publicly confirms an incident consistent with the reported event and provides an affected-individuals count >= 500,000, via SSA statements, OIG report, congressional notification summary, or a formal breach notice.
monitoring_signals: SSA and SSA-OIG press releases
Congressional letters/hearings referencing affected counts
HHS OCR / federal breach notifications (if applicable) and major outlet confirmations
predictions.rationale: Large-scale federal data incidents sometimes lead to quantified notifications, but investigations can stay non-public or omit counts.

predictions.text: The U.S. Department of Education will publish a Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPRM) in the Federal Register proposing a new or substantially revised income-driven repayment framework following the termination of the SAVE plan.
predictions.verification_criteria: Federal Register publication of an NPRM by the Department of Education that explicitly proposes (i) a new IDR plan or (ii) material revisions to IDR rules, and references replacing/responding to the end of SAVE or related court decisions.
monitoring_signals: Unified Agenda / OMB regulatory docket entries for ED
Federal Register rulemaking publications
ED press releases and guidance updates to servicers
predictions.rationale: Policy pressure to offer an IDR alternative is high, but timelines for drafting and clearing rulemaking are uncertain.

predictions.text: At least one of Blackstone, Apollo, Ares, or KKR will disclose a quarterly net asset value (NAV) decline of at least 2.0% in a flagship private-credit vehicle primarily due to credit marks/spread widening (not distributions), in an investor report or public filing.
predictions.verification_criteria: An investor report, fund quarterly report, or the sponsor’s public disclosure (10-Q/10-K, earnings deck) stating NAV (or NAV per share) fell by >= 2.0% over the quarter and attributing the decline primarily to mark-to-market/valuation changes on credit holdings (rather than net distributions).
monitoring_signals: Quarterly reports for BDCs/credit interval funds affiliated with these sponsors
Manager earnings calls discussing valuation marks and non-accruals
Secondary-market pricing for private-credit vehicles and CLO mezz tranches
predictions.rationale: If volatility persists, valuation pressure is plausible; however, reporting lags and smoothing practices reduce near-term probability.

predictions.text: At least one top-5 global container shipping line (by capacity) will publicly announce suspension/avoidance of transits through the Strait of Hormuz for at least 7 consecutive days due to security risk.
predictions.verification_criteria: Official company statement (press release, advisory, verified social media, or customer notice reported by major media) from a top-5 carrier stating Hormuz transits are suspended/avoided for security reasons, with the suspension lasting >= 7 consecutive days (as indicated by dated advisories).
monitoring_signals: Carrier customer advisories and route updates
AIS-based route deviations reported by maritime analytics firms
UKMTO/IMSC incident bulletins and threat-level changes
predictions.rationale: Escalation raises maritime risk, but container lines may reroute selectively without formal multi-day suspensions, keeping confidence moderate.

predictions.text: The Federal Reserve will terminate (or announce termination of) at least one additional formal enforcement action against a top-20 foreign banking organization’s U.S. operations.
predictions.verification_criteria: Federal Reserve 'Enforcement Actions' press release or listing showing termination of an action involving a top-20 foreign banking organization’s U.S. operations, dated on or before the deadline.
monitoring_signals: Federal Reserve enforcement-action updates
Bank disclosures about remediation milestones and consent-order terminations
Regulatory exam-cycle and MRAs/MRIs commentary in filings
predictions.rationale: Recent terminations suggest a broader cleanup cycle, but the pace depends on supervisory findings and remediation verification.

predictions.text: Intel will announce at least one paid commercial deployment (customer purchase, contracted pilot, or revenue-generating product availability) for a fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) hardware accelerator or FHE-enabled chip feature, with a named customer or partner.
predictions.verification_criteria: Intel press release, earnings materials, or partner/customer announcement stating (i) a commercial deployment/paid pilot or product availability for FHE acceleration and (ii) naming at least one customer or ecosystem partner involved.
monitoring_signals: Intel product announcements and roadmap events
Hyperscaler/security-vendor partnership releases
Mentions of FHE acceleration in Intel earnings calls or product briefs
predictions.rationale: Technical demos can take time to productize; a named, paid deployment is plausible but not assured within the year.
